title:
Lesson 1:
It's Hot
description: 'Hava sıcak' = It's hot (weather). 'Ne sıcak!' = How hot! / So hot! 'Sıcak' = It's hot (for objects/food). Note: use 'hava sıcak' for weather, just 'sıcak' for things.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: How hot! I can't go out like this.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Evet, hava çok sıcak bugün. Kırk derece.
dialogue.english: Yes, the weather is very hot today. Forty degrees.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Teşekkürler ama çok sıcak. Soğuk su tercih ederim.
dialogue.english: Thanks but it's too hot. I prefer cold water.

title:
Lesson 2:
It's Cold / I'm Cold
description: 'Hava soğuk' = It's cold (weather). 'Ne soğuk!' = How cold! / So cold! 'Üşüyorum' = I'm cold / I feel cold. Use 'hava soğuk' for the weather, 'üşüyorum' for your own feeling.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.turkish: Evet, hava çok soğuk. Üşüyor musun?
dialogue.english: Yes, the weather is very cold. Are you cold?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I'm cold. I forgot to bring my coat.
